project.title: Modification of Human MAVS protein
project.projectDescription: This modification plays a critical role in regulating protein function and cellular processes.MAVS succination sites were identified using mass spectrometry (MS). Identify succination sites of human MAVS protein.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: HEK293 cells were transfected with MAVS-expressing plasmids for 24 hours. Immunoprecipitated MAVS was subjected to SDS-PAGE, and the gel band corresponding to MAVS was excised, reduced with DTT, alkylated with iodoacetamide, and digested with sequencing-grade trypsin overnight at 37°C. The resulting peptides were analyzed by LC-MS/MS. The digestion products were separated on a C18 column using an 85-minute gradient at 0.300 µL/min flow rate, with a Thermo-Dionex Ultimate 3000 HPLC system coupled to a Thermo Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS data were acquired in data-dependent mode with a full scan (200-1600 m/z, 60,000 resolution) in the Orbitrap. The MS/MS spectra were searched against MAVS.fasta using Proteome Discoverer (Version PD2.5). Search parameters included trypsin digestion (2 missed cleavages allowed), carbamidomethylation (C), oxidation (M), and succinocysteine (C) as variable modifications. Precursor ion mass tolerance was set to 20 ppm, and fragment ion mass tolerance was 0.02 Da. Confidence was set to 1% FDR.HEK293 cells were transfected with MAVS-expressing plasmids for 24 hours, followed by treatment with 10 μM DMF for 6 hours. Immunoprecipitated MAVS was subjected to SDS-PAGE, and the gel band corresponding to MAVS was excised, reduced with DTT, alkylated with iodoacetamide, and digested with sequencing-grade trypsin overnight at 37°C. The resulting peptides were analyzed by LC-MS/MS. The digestion products were separated on a C18 column using an 85-minute gradient at 0.300 µL/min flow rate, with a Thermo-Dionex Ultimate 3000 HPLC system coupled to a Thermo Orbitrap Exploris 480 mass spectrometer. MS data were acquired in data-dependent mode with a full scan (200-1600 m/z, 60,000 resolution) in the Orbitrap. The MS/MS spectra were searched against MAVS.fasta using Proteome Discoverer (Version PD2.5). Search parameters included trypsin digestion (2 missed cleavages allowed), carbamidomethylation (C), oxidation (M), and succinocysteine (C) as variable modifications. Precursor ion mass tolerance was set to 20 ppm, and fragment ion mass tolerance was 0.02 Da. Confidence was set to 1% FDR.

references.referenceLine: Xia W, Mao Y, Xia Z, Cheng J, Jiang P. Metabolic remodelling produces fumarate via the aspartate-argininosuccinate shunt in macrophages as an antiviral defence. Nat Microbiol. 2025 10(5):1115-1129
